name: The Trabant Queue
description: The vehicle processing lane on the eastern approach — a long queue of idling Trabants whose drivers have abandoned their cars and joined the pedestrian crowd, leaving the vehicles running. The two-stroke engines produce a continuous blue haze of exhaust that mingles with the fog and the floodlight glare.
atmosphericDetails: Dozens of Trabants idling unattended, their headlights on, engines running — the vehicles of a population that has decided the car no longer matters
The distinctive smell of two-stroke exhaust — a smell so specific to East Germany that it will become, for a generation, the smell of this night
Personal items visible through car windows — a child's stuffed toy on a back seat, a thermos of coffee on a dashboard, a map of West Berlin open on the passenger seat
